message:
Harden audit and lease signature hashing with length-prefixed binary encoding

Ecosystem registry (ecosystem_registry.rs):
- Replace format!()-based string concatenation in compute_audit_hash()
  with length-prefixed binary encoding. The old approach concatenated
  fields with colon delimiters (e.g., "seq:prev:ts:ext:event:detail"),
  which is ambiguous when field values themselves contain colons —
  an attacker could craft an extension_id containing ":" to collide
  with a different (sequence, prev_hash) combination. The new encoding
  writes the sequence as fixed-width le_bytes, then each string field
  as a u64 length prefix followed by raw bytes, making the hash
  domain-unambiguous regardless of field content.
- Retain the "ecosystem_registry_hash_v1:" domain separator prefix.

Lease coordinator (lease_coordinator.rs):
- Apply the same length-prefixed encoding fix to verify_quorum() and
  compute_test_signature(). The old "signer_id:content_hash" format
  used a colon delimiter that could be spoofed if a signer_id contained
  ":". Now each field is preceded by its u64 byte-length, eliminating
  the delimiter-injection vector while keeping the
  "lease_coord_sign_v1:" domain prefix.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
